content_body:
"The Land of a Thousand Waterfalls" is what the Cherokee called this land in North Georgia, and Helton Creek Falls in the Chattahoochee National Forests is one of the best waterfalls around. The turn-off to Helton Creek Road is just 1.1 miles from Vogel State Park, so making a day around the park and this waterfall is a wonderful fulfilling family outing. 

From the small parking lot on Helton Creek Road, the trail is well-marked by a sign to the right (west) of the parking area. Enter the woods on a gentle descending trail to a mountain cove, then to the creek bed. The trail to both the falls is about 200 yards. It is just 200 steps where the trail splits to go left for the lower waterfall; the falls are immediately visible, down a fairly steep and well-used set of stairs. To see the upper waterfall, return to the main trail, climb the stairs, then cross a wet-weather branch while heading upstream that leads to the upper waterfall and observation deck with benches.

The lower falls drop over a 30' ledge, tumbling to a pool below. There is a nice flat rock area for a picnic or to stretch out on the rocks with a nice swimming hole. The second upper fall is a tall, full waterfall that drops at least 60 feet. These falls are especially beautiful after a good rainfall. The observation deck gives you a great view.
title: Helton Creek Falls
